name: Nayarit (Mexico)

colors.symbolism: the field and details of the arms

description: A white field with the coat of arms of the state of Nayarit centred.
history: Nayarit has no officially adopted state flag; the design shown — a white field bearing the state coat of arms — is in de facto use to represent the state.

features.symbolism: A white field charged with the state coat of arms, in the typical pattern of Mexican state flags.

The arms feature the figure of an indigenous warrior — the Cora chief Nayar (Nayarit), namesake of the state — drawing a bow, set against landscape elements representing the Pacific coast, the Sierra and the agricultural land of the state.
